title: Lithocardium heterophyllum
taxonomy: Lithocardium heterophyllum is classified as a plant species within the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the figwort family. This family comprises around 240 genera and 4,500 species, many of which are herbs, shrubs, or vines. Lithocardium heterophyllum is native to South America and is known for its medicinal properties. The classification of Lithocardium heterophyllum is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. This plant's classification highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
uses: Lithocardium heterophyllum is also known as the Indian or Asiatic snakeroot, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal properties include being used to treat various digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. It has also been used to treat fever, and in some cases, it has been applied topically to reduce inflammation. The plant contains compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential side effects.

It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, bronchitis, and digestive issues. constituents: Lithocardium heterophyllum, also known as Carduus nutans, is a plant species used in traditional medicine. Its active constituents include flavonoids, such as quercetin, and phenolic acids. The plant also contains other bioactive compounds like saponins, terpenoids, and volatile oils. side_effects: Lithocardium heterophyllum, commonly known as Indian heart leaf, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
